title: Spicy Light Spanish Chicken Paella
description: A romantic and flavorful Spanish dish, perfect for a date night, featuring tender sous-vide chicken, spicy chorizo, and a hint of saffron. This dish is nut-free friendly and light, making it an excellent choice for a special evening.
instructions: Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Seal the chicken in a sous-vide bag and cook for 2 hours.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced chorizo and cook until browned, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the Arborio rice to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, saffron threads, and cooked chicken to the skillet.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the lemon juice and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with lemon wedges if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
